_status: partially populated (2026-07-07 second + third research pass) - see data-requirements.md §17/§18 and ISSUES.md ISS-9/ISS-3

purchaseCost.note: Single Indian government-hospital project estimate (2022), configuration (single-plane vs biplane) unspecified. International USD pricing provides supplementary context only, NOT converted to INR per this project's rule against silent currency conversion: new single-plane $1-2M, new biplane $2-4M (S27); refurbished $200k-1.2M depending on configuration (S27,S35). Indian landed cost would add import duty/GST/logistics not reflected in these USD figures.

usefulLifeYears.note: Companies Act Schedule II's only other category besides the named diagnostic-scan class (Cat-scan/ultrasound, 13yr) is 'other medical/surgical equipment' at 15 years. Cath lab isn't individually named by S8, but as interventional (not imaging-diagnostic) equipment it falls in this second category by elimination -- a direct inference from S8's own two-way split, not a separate research claim. See data-requirements.md §12.4/§14.

salvageValuePercentage.note: Companies Act Schedule II caps residual value at 5% of original cost unless separately justified. See data-requirements.md §18.2 for the citation-correction caveat (same as MRI).

installationAndAncillaryCostPercentage.note: No tender states a fixed percentage. J&K MSCL tender requires turnkey installation plus lab/waiting-area renovation within 180 days; 20-30% is the research pass's own inference from the scope of turnkey work described, not a stated figure -- weaker evidence than a directly quoted percentage. See data-requirements.md §18.3.

warrantyYears.note: AIIMS Rishikesh and J&K MSCL tenders both specify 5-year warranty + 5yr CMC; West Bengal's tender specifies only 2-year warranty + 8yr CMC (trading a shorter warranty for a longer CMC schedule). Genuine tender-to-tender variation. See data-requirements.md §18.1.

cmcYears.note: AIIMS/J&K tenders: 5yr CMC following 5yr warranty. West Bengal tender: 8yr CMC following 2yr warranty. 6.5 is a straight bracket midpoint, not a weighted 'most likely' figure. See data-requirements.md §18.4/§18.8.

amcAnnualCostPercentage.unit: percent of equipment cost per year
amcAnnualCostPercentage.note: Labour-only AMC. Generic tender clause (SCTIMST) applied identically across all 5 equipment types in the absence of Cath-Lab-specific AMC data. See data-requirements.md §18.4.

cmcAnnualCostPercentage.unit: percent of equipment + turnkey cost per year
cmcAnnualCostPercentage.note: The strongest CMC figure of all five equipment types -- a real, detailed, year-wise CMC rate schedule from the West Bengal cath-lab tender: 6% in year 1, rising 0.1 percentage points/year to 6.7% in year 8. Unlike MRI/CT/Dialysis/Ultrasound, this is genuinely Cath-Lab-specific, not a generic proxy. See data-requirements.md §18.4.

_usagePerDayRange.note: Vendor guidance, not empirical. Blends two different procedure types: 4-6 diagnostic catheterizations/day OR 2-4 PCI/interventional procedures/day. Consistent with data-requirements.md §13.3's existing caution that cath lab needs procedure-mix modeling, not one blended average.

billedTariffPerUse.note: Diagnostic cardiac catheterization only (does NOT cover PCI/angioplasty or other interventional procedures -- see the existing usagePerDay note on procedure-mix modeling below). CGHS Oct 2025 rate list (Tier 3/District Rs11,920 to Tier 1/Metro Rs14,900, NABH) and PM-JAY Health Benefit Package (Tier 3 Rs12,500 to Tier 1 Rs15,000) closely agree -- two independent official government schemes. Independently re-verified this session by fetching cghshospitals.com directly (a different site than the original citation), which returned the identical Rs14,900/Rs13,410/Rs11,920 figures. This resolves what was previously a completely empty field -- Cath Lab had zero tariff data through two prior research passes. See data-requirements.md §18.5.

launchDelayMonths.note: Vendor guide breakdown: 3-6mo room construction + 2-4wk equipment delivery + 2-4wk installation/calibration + 1-2wk commissioning/training. Not empirical, but a detailed, plausible breakdown.
